name: Backup and Recovery
description: OT backup and recovery is fundamentally different from IT backup. Restoring a SCADA server is only step one — the system must then be synchronised with the physical plant state before it can resume control. Recovery planning must include operations engineering, not just IT.

requirements.name: OT configuration backup
requirements.description: Back up all OT system configurations, programs, and data that are required to restore OT functionality: PLC ladder logic, DCS configuration, HMI screens/tags, SCADA database, historian configuration, network device configurations.

technical.summary: Scheduled automated backup of all recoverable OT configuration. Offline/isolated backup copies. Include all components in backup scope.
actions: Define backup scope — every OT system must be included: PLC programs, DCS configuration, HMI application, SCADA database, historian configuration, network device configs, engineering tool databases
Automate backups where possible (scheduled SCADA database backup, historian backup)
For PLCs and RTUs: upload and save current program after any change; store in version-controlled repository
Store backup copies on isolated media (not connected to production OT network or internet)
Offsite or offline copy for disaster recovery (NAS at separate site, encrypted cloud backup for non-safety-critical configs)
Test backup restoration quarterly — a backup that cannot be restored is not a backup

governance.summary: Backup completion monitored. Failed backups escalated. Quarterly restore test.
actions: Backup status reported monthly to OT manager
Annual backup scope review — add new assets, remove decommissioned assets

slMapping.sl2: Automated scheduled backup; offline copy; quarterly restore test
slMapping.sl3: Immutable backup storage; restore test includes process reconstitution steps; RTO defined
slMapping.sl4: Real-time backup for critical configs; warm standby; restore tested annually under simulated incident conditions

requirements.name: OT cyber incident recovery procedure
requirements.description: Document and test OT recovery procedures specific to cyber incidents — including how to safely restore control systems to a known-good state while the plant is running, and how to coordinate with operations engineering.

technical.summary: Cyber incident recovery procedure covers: isolated restoration environment, integrity verification before reconnection to OT network, synchronisation with plant state, step-by-step restoration with operations sign-off.
actions: Develop OT cyber incident recovery procedure covering: 1) Isolate affected system 2) Verify integrity of backup 3) Restore from verified backup in isolated environment 4) Test functionality before reconnecting 5) Coordinate with operations on plant state 6) Reconnect with enhanced monitoring 7) Verify normal operation
Include operations engineering in recovery procedure (they control the physical process)
Define Recovery Time Objective (RTO) per OT system based on operational criticality
Test recovery procedure annually — tabletop minimum; full recovery test preferred
Include NACSA s26 notification requirements in recovery procedure timeline

governance.summary: Recovery procedure approved by OT manager and operations manager. Annual test. Post-incident update.
actions: Annual recovery test with documented results
RTO targets reviewed against operational risk annually

evidenceItems: Operations engineering sign-off on recovery procedure

slMapping.sl3: Tested procedure with operations engineering; RTO defined and tested
slMapping.sl4: Hot/warm standby for critical systems; recovery tested under simulated cyber attack conditions
